data.name: Login_Error_Unauthorized
data.value: Your email is not authorized to access this application.

data.name: Login_Button_Oidc
data.comment: {0} = the configured OIDC provider display name.

data.name: Login_NoProvider
data.value: No login method is available. Configure a provider or enable local login.

data.name: Setup_Welcome_Body
data.value: Let's set up your instance. This takes about a minute.

data.name: Setup_Admin_FederatedHint
data.value: This email will be granted admin access. Sign in with Google or OIDC after setup.

data.name: Setup_Security_McpKeyHint
data.value: Copy it now — it won't be shown again. Manage keys later in Settings → MCP.

data.name: Setup_Security_VaultKeyHint
data.value: Optional but recommended. Set this value as the VAULT_KEY environment variable and restart to encrypt stored secrets at rest. It is intentionally not saved to disk (keeping the key off the data volume).

data.name: Setup_Server_Body
data.value: You can add remote servers from the Servers page after signing in.

data.name: Setup_Done_Body
data.value: Finish to create your admin account and sign in.

data.name: AgentHistory_Header_Subtitle
data.value: Every agent/MCP tool call — actor, parameters, verdict, result

data.name: AgentView_Alert_NotEnabled
data.value: The agent is not enabled. Set

data.name: AgentView_Alert_NotEnabledSuffix
data.value: , a provider and an API key in the configuration.

data.name: AgentView_Chat_EmptyState
data.value: Tell the agent what to do — e.g. "list all unhealthy containers".

data.name: AgentView_Header_Subtitle
data.value: Acting assistant — performs actions only within the guardrails and never beyond your own permissions.

data.name: AgentView_Provider_ModelHelperEmpty
data.value: Enter freely, or test the API key for suggestions

data.name: AgentView_Snackbar_AdminOnly
data.value: Only admins may change the agent settings.

data.name: AgentView_Snackbar_FileTooLarge
data.value: {0} is too large (max. 512 KB of text).

data.name: AgentView_SystemPrompt_Hint
data.value: Instruction for the LLM. Empty = default. Does NOT change the guardrails — they are code-enforced at the tool boundary, no matter what is written here.

data.name: AppStore_Alert_PortConflicts
data.value: Port conflicts detected! Please choose different ports.

data.name: AppStore_Empty_SearchPrompt
data.value: Enter a search term and press Enter to find container images across the marketplaces.

data.name: AppStore_Hint_NoFullTextSearch
data.value: This marketplace does not offer full-text search — enter the exact reference (e.g.

data.name: AppStore_Hint_PortFormat
data.value: Format host:container, separate multiple entries with commas.

data.name: AppStore_Snackbar_InvalidProjectName
data.value: Invalid project name. Only letters, digits, dots, hyphens and underscores are allowed.

data.name: CloudView_Confirm_HardReset
data.value: Hard-reset '{0}'? Forced restart without a clean shutdown — data loss possible.

data.name: CloudView_Confirm_Reboot
data.value: Reboot server '{0}'? Running services will be interrupted.

data.name: CloudView_Confirm_Shutdown
data.value: Shut down server '{0}'? The server will be offline afterwards.

data.name: CloudView_Empty_Prefix
data.value: No server with a configured cloud provider. Set the provider + API key per server on the

data.name: CloudView_Header_Subtitle
data.value: Out-of-band power & snapshots via Hetzner / Hostinger — set the provider per server under “Servers”

data.name: ComposeEditor_Empty_NoSelection
data.value: Select a service on the left or create a new one.

data.name: ComposeEditor_Empty_NoServices
data.value: Add services to build a compose file.

data.name: ComposeEditor_Header_Subtitle
data.value: Edit and deploy Docker Compose files

data.name: ComposeEditor_Snackbar_InvalidProjectName
data.value: Invalid project name. Only letters, digits, dots, hyphens and underscores are allowed.

data.name: ContainerDetail_Alert_NotFound
data.value: was not found on any server — it may have been removed or recreated (an image update assigns a new ID).

data.name: ContainerDetail_Cve_NoFindings
data.value: No CVE findings in the image.

data.name: ContainerDetail_Cve_NoScan
data.value: No CVE scan for this container yet — the CVE monitor must be enabled and have run at least once.

data.name: ContainerDetail_Db_NoFrameworkUpload
data.value: No framework detected. Use the SQL file upload instead.

data.name: ContainerDetail_Db_QbDeleteWarning
data.value: DELETE without WHERE deletes ALL rows! WHERE is required.

data.name: ContainerDetail_Db_QbUpdateWarning
data.value: UPDATE without WHERE changes ALL rows! WHERE is required.

data.name: ContainerDetail_Dialog_ExecuteSqlFileMessage
data.value: Execute file '{0}' against the database? This changes data/schema.

data.name: ContainerDetail_Dialog_RemoveMessage
data.value: Really remove container '{0}'? This action cannot be undone.

data.name: ContainerDetail_Dialog_RollbackMessage
data.value: Roll back the last migration? This can lead to data loss.

data.name: ContainerDetail_Dialog_RunMigrationMessage
data.value: Run database migrations? This changes the database schema.

data.name: ContainerDetail_Dialog_SaveEnvMessage
data.value: The .env file will be overwritten and the container restarted. Continue?

data.name: ContainerDetail_Env_DotEnvWarning
data.value: Changes to the .env file require a container restart (brief downtime).

data.name: ContainerDetail_Env_MaskedInfo
data.value: Sensitive values (keys, secrets, passwords) are masked for security reasons.

data.name: ContainerDetail_Env_NoCompose
data.value: No Docker Compose project detected – the .env file cannot be edited.

data.name: ContainerDetail_Env_NoDotEnvFound
data.value: No .env file found. You can create a new one.

data.name: ContainerDetail_Env_SaveSuccess
data.value: Environment variables saved and container restarted.

data.name: ContainerDetail_Health_NoData
data.value: No health data for this container yet.

data.name: ContainerDetail_Stats_OnlyRunning
data.value: Statistics are only available for running containers.

data.name: ContainerDetail_Terminal_MtlsUnavailable
data.value: The container terminal is not available on mTLS servers (the Docker proxy does not forward an interactive stream).

data.name: ContainerDetail_Terminal_OnlyRunning
data.value: The terminal is only available for running containers.

data.name: ContainerDiff_SelectTwo
data.value: Select two containers to compare them.

data.name: ContainerDiff_Subtitle
data.value: Compare the configuration and environment of two containers

data.name: Dashboard_Cve_Tooltip
data.value: {0} Critical, {1} High, {2} Medium, {3} Low — click to open /cves

data.name: Dashboard_Dialog_RemoveMessage
data.value: Really remove container '{0}'? This action cannot be undone.

data.name: Dashboard_Dialog_RollbackMessage
data.value: {0} will be reverted to the previous image (snapshot from {1}). The container will be recreated for this.

data.name: Dashboard_Empty_NoContainers
data.value: No containers found. Please make sure Docker is running and the socket is reachable.

data.name: Dashboard_FirstServer_ClassicText
data.value: Classic connection without the mesh: Docker Remote API (TCP, optional TLS) or an SSH tunnel. For users who prefer not to use Tailscale — you can switch to the mesh at any time.

data.name: Dashboard_FirstServer_ClassicTitle
data.value: Connect via Docker API / SSH only

data.name: Dashboard_FirstServer_OnboardText
data.value: A one-time SSH bootstrap connection installs the Tailscale mesh, telemetry, and the mTLS Docker proxy — after that, the SSH access is deleted. Control, data, and telemetry planes run separately over the mesh. Prerequisite: a free Tailscale account.

data.name: Dashboard_FirstServer_Subtitle
data.value: The local Docker host is connected ✓ — now connect your fleet.

data.name: Dashboard_Header_Subtitle
data.value: Real-time status of all servers and containers

data.name: Dashboard_Table_NoContainers
data.value: No containers found in this project.

data.name: Deploy_Header_Subtitle
data.value: Create and deploy new containers and stacks

data.name: HealthReports_Table_NoRecords
data.value: No health data available yet. The monitor collects data every 30 seconds.

data.name: SelfBackupPanel_Alert_Encrypted
data.value: Backups are encrypted with your VAULT_KEY (AES-256-GCM). The same VAULT_KEY is required to restore.

data.name: SelfBackupPanel_Alert_Postgres
data.value: This instance uses PostgreSQL. The backup contains configuration and secrets under /app/data, but not the PostgreSQL database itself — back it up separately (e.g. with pg_dump).

data.name: SelfBackupPanel_Alert_Unencrypted
data.value: VAULT_KEY is not set — backups are stored unencrypted and contain plaintext secrets (ssh keys, mtls, API keys, DataProtection ring). Set VAULT_KEY for encrypted backups, or store downloaded files securely.

data.name: SelfBackupPanel_Dialog_RestoreMessage
data.value:
Restore {0}?
This REPLACES ALL current data (database, configuration, keys), automatically creates a safety backup first, and restarts Whiskers. All sessions will be signed out. If Whiskers runs without Docker/systemd, you must restart the process manually afterwards.

data.name: SelfBackupPanel_Snackbar_RestoreRunning
data.value: Restore in progress — Whiskers will restart shortly…

data.name: SelfBackupPanel_Text_Intro
data.value: Backs up Whiskers' own state under /app/data (database, configuration, keys) into a downloadable archive. A restore replaces all data and restarts Whiskers.

data.name: SelfBackupPanel_Text_UploadInfo
data.value: Upload a previously downloaded backup file. Whiskers first automatically creates a safety backup of the current state, then replaces all data and restarts.

data.name: SelfBackupPanel_Tooltip_ScheduleBackups
data.value: Set up recurring self-backups as a scheduled task (type „Whiskers self-backup“)

data.name: Servers_Action_Onboard
data.value: Onboard into the mesh (Tailscale + mTLS, SSH-free)

data.name: Servers_CloudKey_HelperKeep
data.value: A key is stored — leave empty to keep it.

data.name: Servers_CloudKey_HelperNew
data.value: The provider's API token (Hetzner: project token; Hostinger: API token). Matching is done via the server IP.

data.name: Servers_ConnectError_Hint
data.value: Check host/port/TLS — the server remains saved; correct it and save again.

data.name: Servers_ConnectError_Prefix
data.value: Saved, but the connection could not be established:

data.name: Servers_Dialog_DeleteMessage
data.value: Really remove server '{0}'? The connection and stored credentials will be lost.

data.name: Servers_Dialog_TailscaleMessage
data.value:
One-click onboarding connects the server through a Tailscale mesh (SSH-free via mTLS afterwards). This requires a — free — Tailscale account; the login link appears during onboarding.

Don't want Tailscale? Then simply connect the server the classic way with “Save” via the Docker API (TCP/TLS) or SSH — without the mesh.

data.name: Servers_Field_KubeNamespaces_Helper
data.value: Empty = all visible namespaces. Example: default, apps, monitoring

data.name: Servers_Field_SshPassword_Helper
data.value: Used only once for onboarding, never stored, and discarded on success.

data.name: Servers_Field_UseSshPassword
data.value: Use root/password instead of an SSH key (first-time onboarding only)

data.name: Servers_KubeRbacHint
data.value: Recommendation: use a minimal ServiceAccount instead of the admin kubeconfig — manifest + instructions at

data.name: Servers_KubeVaultWarning_Prefix
data.value: Kubernetes clusters require the secret vault: the kubeconfig is stored encrypted in the vault (never as a file). Please set

data.name: Servers_Kubeconfig_HelperKeep
data.value: Leave empty to keep the stored kubeconfig.

data.name: Servers_Kubeconfig_HelperVault
data.value: Stored encrypted in the vault — never on disk.

data.name: Servers_OnboardInfo
data.value: Onboarding (go SSH-free): “Save & Onboard” installs Tailscale + the mTLS proxy over one SSH bootstrap connection and then switches the host to TCP+mTLS. The key or password is removed automatically on success.

data.name: Servers_Onboard_Description
data.value: Installs Tailscale, deploys telemetry + the mTLS proxy, and makes the server SSH-free (mTLS).

data.name: Servers_Onboard_IdempotentHint
data.value: All steps are idempotent — “Retry” resumes at the failed step.

data.name: Servers_Onboard_TailscaleLoginHint
data.value: Tailscale login — open it in the browser and approve the node:

data.name: Servers_TailscaleSsh_Hint
data.value: The web terminal connects keylessly through the tailnet (mesh identity, no SSH key) as root on the host from the field above. Requirement:

data.name: Settings_Alert_AiChatInfo
data.value: Read-only advisor chat (no actions). The API key is stored in plain text under /app/data — same as servers.json.

data.name: Settings_Alert_ApiKeyLevels
data.value: Each API key has a permission level: Read (queries only), Write (manage containers/servers), or Admin (all commands incl. shell).

data.name: Settings_Alert_EnvInfo
data.value: These values come from environment variables (.env) and only take effect after a restart — shown here for reference only. The host binding (HOST_BIND/HOST_PORT) lives at Compose level and is not visible inside the container.

data.name: Settings_Alert_NoAccess
data.value: No access — this page requires administrator rights. Your role: {0}.

data.name: Settings_Alert_VaultDisabled
data.value: The vault is disabled. Set the VAULT_KEY environment variable to store secrets encrypted.

data.name: Settings_Alert_WhitelistEmpty
data.value: The whitelist is enabled but empty — nobody can sign in!

data.name: Settings_Label_MetricAlertsEnabled
data.value: Metric alerts enabled (emits high_cpu / high_memory / high_disk / metric_anomaly)

data.name: Settings_Label_NotifyOnUpdate
data.value: Notify when an update is available

data.name: Settings_Snackbar_ApiKeyCreated
data.value: API key '{0}' created. Copy it and store it safely!

data.name: Settings_Snackbar_CveScanStarted
data.value: CVE scan started — the first run can take several minutes (Trivy DB download).

data.name: Settings_Snackbar_McpSaved
data.value: MCP permissions saved. Changes take effect immediately.

data.name: Settings_Snackbar_TerminalSaved
data.value: Terminal saved (applies immediately to new sessions).

data.name: Settings_Snackbar_TestSent
data.value: Test notification sent to all configured channels (save first!).

data.name: Settings_Snackbar_WhitelistEmptyError
data.value: An enabled whitelist without entries would lock everyone out. Add at least one email or disable the whitelist.

data.name: Settings_Snackbar_WhitelistSaved
data.value: Whitelist saved. Changes apply from the next sign-in.

data.name: Servers_Error_KubeNeedsVault
data.value: Kubernetes clusters require the vault — set VAULT_KEY and restart.

data.name: AiChat_Empty_Advisor
data.value: Ask me something about your containers, servers, or databases.

data.name: AiChat_Empty_Agent
data.value: I can see what's on your page — tell me what to do.

data.name: AiChat_Input_AgentPlaceholder
data.value: Task… (Enter to send, Shift+Enter for a new line)

data.name: AiTriggersView_AdminOnly_Warning
data.value: Only admins can manage AI triggers.

data.name: AiTriggersView_Empty
data.value: No triggers yet. Use "New trigger" to add one.

data.name: AiTriggersView_Intro
data.value: Events that launch the agent autonomously. Per trigger: event type(s), an optional name filter, a dedicated task/workflow, and the guardrail preset that constrains the run. In an autonomous run, confirmations up to the preset level are approved automatically. Admins only.

data.name: AiTriggersView_LogRuleCreated_Snackbar
data.value: Log alert rule 'Fehler/Errors' created — fires on errors in container logs.

data.name: AiTriggersView_Prompt_Placeholder
data.value: e.g. "Check the logs of the affected container, identify the cause, and restart it if it is a transient error. Summarize the cause."

data.name: AiTriggersView_TemplateAdded_Snackbar
data.value: Template '{0}' added — don't forget to save.

data.name: AiTriggersView_Templates_Caption
data.value: Quick templates (add a prefilled trigger — then click "Save"):

data.name: ApprovalsView_Empty_Hint
data.value: As soon as an agent wants to run an action that requires confirmation, it will appear here.

data.name: ApprovalsView_NoPermission_Snackbar
data.value: No permission to approve this action — your role is lower than the requested permission level.

data.name: ApprovalsView_Subtitle
data.value: Actions the agent needs your confirmation for

data.name: ChatWidget_Chart_NoData
data.value: No metric data for the last hour.

data.name: Cves_NoRecords
data.value: No CVEs (or nothing matches the filter).

data.name: Cves_Snackbar_ScanStarted
data.value: CVE scan started — the first run can take several minutes (Trivy DB download).

data.name: Cves_Summary
data.value: {0} unique CVEs (of {1} total) — one row per CVE, expand for affected containers/servers.

data.name: Cves_Tooltip_Unverified
data.value: Pending security update without a resolved CVE ID (lower confidence)

data.name: Error_DevMode_Info
data.value: Switching to the Development environment displays more detailed information about the error that occurred.

data.name: Error_DevMode_Warning
data.value: The Development environment shouldn't be enabled for deployed applications. It can result in displaying sensitive information from exceptions to end users. For local debugging, enable the Development environment by setting the ASPNETCORE_ENVIRONMENT environment variable to Development and restarting the app.

data.name: Error_Subtitle
data.value: An error occurred while processing your request.

data.name: Firewall_Dialog_DisableMessage
data.value: Really disable the firewall? All ports will be opened to incoming connections and the server will be unprotected.

data.name: GitDeployView_Alert_Intro
data.value: The repository is cloned on the target server and built and started with its compose file. Push-triggered redeploys: create a webhook with the "git-deploy" action (HMAC-signed).

data.name: GitDeployView_Alert_NoVault
data.value: Without VAULT_KEY, only public repos are possible (no token storage).

data.name: GitDeployView_Dialog_DeleteMessage
data.value: Really delete '{0}'? The deploy working directory on the target server will be removed; running containers keep running.

data.name: GitDeployView_Empty_NoApps
data.value: No Git deploy apps configured yet.

data.name: GitDeployView_Header_Subtitle
data.value: Drop in a repo URL → build & deploy on the target server

data.name: GitDeployView_HelperText_TokenKeep
data.value: A token is stored in the vault — leave empty to keep it.

data.name: GitDeployView_HelperText_TokenNew
data.value: Stored encrypted in the vault and used via GIT_ASKPASS (never in logs or the process list).

data.name: GitDeployView_Tooltip_PrivateRepo
data.value: Private repo — token in the vault

data.name: GuardrailsView_AdminOnly_Warning
data.value: Only admins can change the guardrails.

data.name: GuardrailsView_Forbidden_Helper
data.value: Regex matched against destructive shell/SQL arguments.

data.name: GuardrailsView_Intro
data.value: Code-enforced, non-bypassable security policy. Enforced at the tool boundary (not in the prompt). Create multiple presets and switch the active one — the agent always uses the active preset. Admins only.

data.name: GuardrailsView_KillSwitch_Caption
data.value: Immediately blocks everything above "read" — regardless of any other setting.

data.name: GuardrailsView_Protected_Helper
data.value: Containers/servers/DBs the agent must never touch — e.g. serverwatch*

data.name: GuardrailsView_ToolHint
data.value: Checked = the agent may use this tool (within level/confirmation limits).

data.name: GuardrailsView_ToolHint_AllowMode
data.value: Whitelist: nothing is allowed except the checked tools.

data.name: GuardrailsView_ToolHint_DenyMode
data.value: Deny: everything is allowed except the unchecked tools.

data.name: Help_Subtitle
data.value: How to use Whiskers — pick a chapter on the left or search above.

data.name: LogSearchView_Header_Subtitle
data.value: Search container logs and set up alerts

data.name: LogSearchView_Rules_Empty
data.value: No alert rules. Create a rule to get notified about specific log patterns.

data.name: ModuleGuard_Alert_Disabled
data.value: This module is disabled. Enable it via Features:{0}:Enabled=true and restart Whiskers.

data.name: Networks_Dialog_DeleteMessage
data.value: Really delete network '{0}'? Connected containers will lose this network connection.

data.name: NginxManager_Dialog_SaveMessage
data.value: Save the configuration for '{0}' and reload Nginx? A faulty configuration can break the served sites.

data.name: ProductionReadinessPanel_Summary
data.value: {0} of {1} checks passed — for secure, reliable production operation.

data.name: ReconnectModal_Text_Failed
data.value:
Connection failed.
Please try again or reload the page.

data.name: ReconnectModal_Text_Paused
data.value: The session was paused by the server.

data.name: ReconnectModal_Text_ResumeFailed
data.value:
The session could not be resumed.
Please try again or reload the page.

data.name: RegistriesPanel_Dialog_DeleteMessage
data.value: Delete registry '{0}' and its vault credential?

data.name: RegistriesPanel_Field_CredentialKeepHelper
data.value: Leave empty to keep the stored credential.

data.name: RegistriesPanel_Field_HostHelper
data.value: As it appears in image references.

data.name: RegistriesPanel_Intro
data.value: Private registries for authenticated pulls: the credential is stored encrypted in the vault and used automatically whenever an image name matches the registry host (e.g. ghcr.io/…).

data.name: RegistriesPanel_Warning_NoVault
data.value: Without VAULT_KEY, registry credentials cannot be stored.

data.name: ScheduledTasksView_Dialog_DeleteMessage
data.value: Really delete scheduled task '{0}'? This action cannot be undone.

data.name: ScheduledTasksView_Empty_NoTasks
data.value: No scheduled tasks yet. Create a new task!

data.name: ScheduledTasksView_Field_CronHelperText
data.value: Format: min hour day month weekday (e.g. 0 2 * * * = daily at 02:00). Times are UTC.

data.name: ScheduledTasksView_Header_Subtitle
data.value: Schedule and manage automated container actions

data.name: SslCerts_NoRecords
data.value: No SSL certificates found. Is certbot installed?

data.name: SystemdManager_Confirm_Restart
data.value: Restart service '{0}'? The service will be briefly interrupted.

data.name: SystemdManager_Confirm_Stop
data.value: Stop service '{0}'? The service and all dependent functionality will be shut down.

data.name: SystemdManager_Subtitle
data.value: Start, stop and monitor systemd units

data.name: VolumeBackupsView_Dialog_DeleteMessage
data.value: Really delete the backup of '{0}' from {1}?

data.name: VolumeBackupsView_Dialog_RestoreMessage
data.value: Restore volume '{0}' from this backup? The current contents of the volume will be overwritten.

data.name: WebhooksView_Alert_Intro
data.value: Webhooks can be called by GitHub, GitLab, or other CI/CD systems to automatically restart or deploy containers.

data.name: WebhooksView_Alert_NoGitApps_Before
data.value: No Git deploy apps yet — first create one under

data.name: WebhooksView_Dialog_DeleteMessage
data.value: Really delete webhook '{0}'? Its URL becomes invalid and can no longer be triggered.

data.name: WebhooksView_Dialog_RegenerateMessage
data.value: Regenerate the secret for '{0}'? The old secret becomes invalid immediately — CI systems must be updated.

data.name: WebhooksView_SecretDialog_Warning
data.value: This secret is shown only NOW and cannot be retrieved later. Store it in your CI system (e.g. as a GitHub webhook secret) — the payload is expected to be signed as X-Hub-Signature-256 (HMAC-SHA256).

data.name: WebhooksView_Tooltip_LegacyNoSecret
data.value: Legacy webhook without a secret — disabled. Regenerate the secret, then re-enable it.
